title: Scorching Heat Grips Odisha: Jharsuguda Tops Nation's Temperature Charts
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
full_text:
Scorching Heat Grips Odisha: Jharsuguda Tops Nation's Temperature Charts
Odisha's Jharsuguda became India's hottest place, reaching 44.6°C. Twenty-five towns in the state saw temperatures soar above 40°C. Significant heatwave alert and school closure measures have been announced in several districts to combat the extreme weather conditions.
On Wednesday, Jharsuguda in Odisha claimed the dubious honor of being the hottest location in India, with temperatures reaching a sweltering 44.6 degrees Celsius, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
The state's meteorological bulletin reported that 25 places recorded temperatures at or above 40 degrees, with four towns surpassing 43 degrees Celsius—among them, Talcher, Sambalpur, Hirakud, and Angul.
Heatwave warnings have been issued for Thursday in Bolangir, Jharsuguda, and Sambalpur, prompting some district administrations to adjust school schedules or issue closures to protect children amid the rising temperatures.
(With inputs from agencies.)

title: Odisha’s Jharsuguda scorches at 44.6 degrees Celsius, hottest in India - Telangana Today
search_term: ("mercury soars" OR "mercury crosses" OR "temperature soars" OR "record temperature" OR "all-time high" OR "degrees Celsius" OR "maximum temperature" OR "hottest day" OR "temperature crosses 40" OR "temperature crosses 45" OR "temperature likely to rise") Odisha
full_text:
Home |India |Odishas Jharsuguda Scorches At 44 6 Degrees Celsius Hottest In India
Odisha’s Jharsuguda scorches at 44.6 degrees Celsius, hottest in India
Odisha’s Jharsuguda recorded the country’s highest temperature at 44.6°C, according to the IMD. Twenty-five other towns crossed the 40°C mark, with several districts including Sambalpur, Angul, and Titlagarh experiencing severe heat. Bhubaneswar touched 41.2°C for the first time this summer.
Bhubaneswar: Odisha’s industrial town of Jharsuguda on Wednesday emerged as the hottest place in the country with mercury level reaching 44.6 degrees Celsius, IMD said.
Twenty-five other places in the state also recorded maximum temperature at or above 40 degrees Celsius, it said.
According to the evening bulletin issued by the Meteorological Centre here, four places in the state recorded a temperature above 43 degrees Celsius during the day. Those places are—Talcher (43.5 degrees Celsius), Sambalpur (43.3), Hirakud (43.2), and Angul (43.1).
Angul was followed by Titlagarh at 42.5 degrees Celsius, Dhenkanal (42.4), Bolangir (41.9), Sonepur (41.9), Sundargarh (41.8), Nuapada (41.6), Bhawanipatna (41.5), Nayagarh (41.5), and Rourkela (41.3), as per the bulletin.
At 41.2 degrees Celsius, capital city Bhubaneswar recorded a temperature above 40 degrees Celsius for the first time during this summer, officials said.
The other places which recorded a day temperature of 40 degrees Celsius or above are—Chandbali, Baripada, Keonjhar, Phulbani, Boudh, Paralakhemundi, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Khurda and Jagatsinghpur, the IMD said.
The weather officer said heatwave conditions are expected to prevail in the districts of Bolangir, Jharsuguda and Sambalpur on Thursday.
Similarly, hot and humid weather is expected to prevail at isolated places over the districts of Bolangir, Sundergarh, Boudh, Sonepur, Sambalpur, Angul, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati districts on Thursday, it said.
There will be no large change in the day temperature during the next four days over the districts of Odisha; thereafter, it is expected to fall by 2-3 degrees Celsius, the bulletin said.
In view of the prevailing heatwave conditions, the Bhadrak district administration has directed the classes of schools during morning hours from 6.30 am to 9 am, while the Sonepur district collector has extended the holiday for all educational institutes for another two days, till April 25.
The district administration in Dhenkanal announced the closure of all schools from Sishu Vatika to Class-12 from Thursday until further order.

title: Schools Shut In More Districts As Heatwave Scorches Odisha; Check IMD Forecast - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Bhubaneswar: More districts in Odisha have announced closure of school from Thursday to ensure safety of students amid the prevailing heatwave conditions.
The Sundargarh and Sambalpur administrations on Wednesday ordered the closure of all government and private schools from April 23 to April 27. Similarly, schools in Kendrapada and Koraput districts will also remain shut from April 23 to April 25.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), heatwave may prevail at isolated places in the districts of Balangir and Jharsuguda on April 22, and Balangir, Jharsuguda and Sambalpur on April 23. Meanwhile, hot and humid conditions may continue in Balangir, Sundergarh, Boudh, Sonepur, Sambalpur, Angul, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapada, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati till April 29. Warm nights are expected at isolated places in Balangir and Kandhamal from April 24-26.
Amid the sultry spell, thunderstorm activity with wind speed of 30-40 kmph may also occur in the afternoon/evening hours in several parts of the state till April 26. Warning has been issued for Rayagada, Malkangiri, Koraput, Nabarangpur, Mayurbhanj, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapada, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati on April 22 and Koraput and Malkangiri on April 23-24.
On April 25, thunderstorm accompanied by lightning and gusty surface wind (40-50 kmph) may occur in the afternoon/evening hours at one or two places in Ganjam, Gajapati and Rayagada. Similar warnings have been sounded for Ganjam, Gajapati and Rayagada the following day.
While there is no warning thereafter, light to moderate rain or thunderstorm may occur at one or two places in the districts of Sundargarh, Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Gajapati and Malkangiri during the subsequent two days.
The weather agency has also predicted a fall in the day temperature by 2-3°C after 4 days.

title: Mercury soars past 40 degrees in Odisha; Jharsuguda highest at 44.4°C, Bhubaneswar at 41°C degrees - Odisha TV
full_text:
Odisha is currently grappling with a severe heatwave, with temperatures soaring across the state. The capital city of Bhubaneswar recorded a high of 41°C by 2:30 PM on Wednesday, causing discomfort for residents and raising concerns about the health risks associated with the extreme heat.
As the heatwave continues, several other districts in the state are also facing unusually high temperatures, with some cities surpassing the 40°C mark.
Meanwhile, Jharsuguda has emerged as the hottest city in the state, reaching an alarming 44.4°C. Other cities such as Sambalpur and Hirakud recorded 43.2°C and 43°C, respectively, while Rourkela and Bhubaneswar both registered 41°C. The oppressive heat is making daily life increasingly difficult, particularly for outdoor workers and vulnerable populations such as the elderly and children.
“Temperatures have exceeded 40°C in 18 districts across Odisha, with Jharsuguda recording a scorching 43.6°C. Rainfall activities accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning, were observed in Koraput district, with 106mm of rain. Looking ahead to the next 24 hours, Mayurbhanj, Koraput, Malkangiri, and Rayagada districts are expected to experience rainfall along with thunderstorms and wind speeds ranging from 30 to 40 km/h. The extreme heatwave and humidity are likely to persist for the next five days in Angul, Sambalpur, Sonepur, Boudh, and Sundargarh districts. The IMD has issued heatwave warnings for the next two days in Jharsuguda and Bolangir, with these areas, along with Sambalpur, likely to continue experiencing heatwave conditions,” said IMD Director Manorama Mohanty.
As temperatures continue to rise, the government has issued advisories to stay hydrated and avoid unnecessary outdoor activities during peak heat hours. The intense heat is expected to persist for the coming days.
